factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in many core markets.

evidence.text: In 2018, we expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent, with slightly higher growth, on a constant currency basis, in the U.S. than Canada.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand in core markets, supported by specific growth figures.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with mixed results.

evidence.text: Rental rates decreased 0.2 percent and increased 0.4 percent year-over-year, on an actual and a pro forma basis, respectively; the volume of OEC on rent increased 18.2 percent and 7.1 percent year-over-year, on an actual and a pro forma basis, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed results with slight decreases and increases in rental rates, indicating stability.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Equipment rentals represented 86 percent of our total revenues. For the year ended December 31, 2017, equipment rental revenue increased 15.7 percent, primarily due to an 18.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent, which includes the impact of the NES and Neff acquisitions, partially offset by a 0.2 percent rental rate decrease.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue is shifting towards higher-margin segments, supported by a significant increase in equipment rental revenue.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating.

evidence.text: On a pro forma basis including the standalone, pre-acquisition results of NES and Neff, equipment rental revenue increased 7.6 percent year-over-year, primarily reflecting a 7.1 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent and a 0.4 percent rental rate increase. We believe that the increase in the volume of OEC on rent reflects improving demand in many of our core markets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is decelerating, as indicated by a lower year-over-year increase compared to previous periods.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 18.2 percent and 7.1 percent year-over-year, on an actual and a pro forma basis, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in unit volumes driven by real demand, supported by significant year-over-year increases.

factors.summary: Construction and industrial markets are growing fastest.

evidence.text: We expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent, with slightly higher growth, on a constant currency basis, in the U.S. than Canada.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction and industrial markets are growing fastest, supported by solid demand expectations.

factors.summary: Margins are compressing due to increased SG&A costs.

evidence.text: For the year ended December 31, 2017, EBITDA margin decreased 270 basis points to 43.6 percent, and adjusted EBITDA margin decreased 30 basis points to 47.6 percent. The decrease in the EBITDA margin primarily reflects i) increased SG&A compensation costs, including stock compensation costs, largely due to the impact of the NES and Neff acquisitions, increased revenue, improved profitability, and increases in our stock price and in the volume of stock awards, and ii) increased merger related costs and restructuring charges associated with the NES and Neff acquisitions.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing due to increased SG&A costs, as evidenced by a significant decrease in EBITDA margins.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering savings.

evidence.text: We continue to implement Lean kaizen processes across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations. We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives, including those included in the Project XL work streams discussed below, in 2017 and 2016, and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering savings, as evidenced by Lean kaizen processes achieving expected savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through Lean initiatives.

evidence.text: We continue to implement Lean kaizen processes across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations. We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives, including those included in the Project XL work streams discussed below, in 2017 and 2016, and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows clear improvement in productivity through Lean initiatives with achieved savings.

factors.summary: Management expects continued growth.

evidence.text: In 2018, we expect to continue our disciplined focus on increasing our profitability and return on invested capital. In particular, our strategy calls for: [...] The pursuit of strategic acquisitions to continue to expand our core equipment rental business, as exhibited by our recently completed acquisitions of NES and Neff.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects continued growth, supported by strategic acquisitions and expansion plans.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by expansion and hiring.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 18 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2018 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings; and [...] The pursuit of strategic acquisitions to continue to expand our core equipment rental business, as exhibited by our recently completed acquisitions of NES and Neff.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's actions, such as opening branches and investing, indicate confidence in growth.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is clear and well-resourced.

evidence.text: Our strategy calls for: [...] The continued expansion of our trench, power and pump footprint, as well as our tools offering, and the cross-selling of these services throughout our network. We plan to open at least 18 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2018 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings; and [...] The pursuit of strategic acquisitions to continue to expand our core equipment rental business, as exhibited by our recently completed acquisitions of NES and Neff.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear, well-resourced strategy with specific goals for expansion and investment.

factors.summary: Macroeconomic factors are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: We expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent, with slightly higher growth, on a constant currency basis, in the U.S. than Canada.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While macroeconomic factors are noted, the evidence still suggests solid demand, indicating a cautious but stable outlook.

factors.summary: Specialty rental branch openings are a near-term catalyst.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 18 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2018 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The planned opening of specialty branches is a near-term catalyst for growth.

factors.summary: Market share is increasing, particularly in specialty segments.

evidence.text: The number of rental locations in our higher margin trench, power and pump (also referred to as 'specialty') segment increased by thirteen year-over-year due to cold starts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is increasing its market share, particularly in specialty segments, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: Differentiation is through scale and customer service.

evidence.text: As a larger company, we have more extensive resources and certain compelling competitive advantages. These include a fleet of rental equipment with a total original equipment cost ('OEC'), based on the initial consideration paid, of $11.5 billion, and a national branch network that operates in 49 U.S. states and every Canadian province, and serves 99 of the 100 largest metropolitan areas in the United States.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's differentiation through scale and customer service provides a competitive advantage.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is slightly decreasing.

evidence.text: 69 percent of equipment rental revenue was derived from key accounts, as compared to 70 percent in 2016. Key accounts are each managed by a single point of contact to enhance customer service.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The slight decrease in customer concentration indicates a positive trend towards diversification.

factors.summary: Capex is increasing to support growth.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 18 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2018 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Planned investments in new branches and fleet expansion suggest a positive outlook for growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving.

evidence.text: These actions have improved our financial flexibility and liquidity and positioned us to invest the necessary capital to take advantage of business opportunities. As of December 31, 2017, we had available liquidity of $1.71 billion, including cash of $352.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved financial flexibility and liquidity indicate a positive development in debt management.

factors.summary: MA activity is creating value through strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: The pursuit of strategic acquisitions to continue to expand our core equipment rental business, as exhibited by our recently completed acquisitions of NES and Neff. Strategic acquisitions allow us to invest our capital to expand our business, further driving our ability to accomplish our strategic goals.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic acquisitions are creating significant value and driving business expansion.
